mentions.note: Planned orthopedics spin-off should lift the core pharma multiple, echoing the Kenvue precedent; today's dip is a terrific entry.
Led the pack among healthcare names Cramer heard from at the JPMorgan Healthcare Conference; thinks it may be undervalued.
Deserves to rally given its recession-resistant business, but the current pace of the move is too fast.

Pure-play pharma after orthopedics spinoff; talc lawsuits no longer a stock-moving factor as company fights claims individually; best drugs in pipeline shine without commoditized knee/hip business holding it back.

Strong pharma growth (15% ex-Stellara), oncology cures (83% progression-free survival multiple myeloma), $5B mental health opportunity (Capilano), solid MedTech cadence, vision care leadership; litigation risks diminishing.
Old-fashioned solid earner benefiting from AI productivity themes; not expensive vs market despite strong runs; dividend and buyback engine.
Consumer staple; real products; on Cramer's list of understandable, in-demand companies.
Terrific drug company; quality pharmaceuticals outperformed during market rotation.
Psoriasis pill FDA approval significant but stock down despite good news; stagflation narrative overpowering positive catalysts; wait for better opportunity.

FDA approval of Icotide (first oral IL-23 antagonist for plaque psoriasis) major win masked by oil selloff; Skyrizi competitor with $22B+ addressable market; legal headwinds (baby powder suits) finally fading.

Owned in travel trust; company transformed shedding slower-growing divisions, focusing on research for life-saving drugs; most blockbusters and best pipeline in industry; often hammered on news release then rallies on conference call.

Best-in-class drug stock (second after Eli Lilly); buying into free fall; new pill psoriasis drug (Icoted) more convenient than needles; talc litigation resolved; orthopedic spin-off unlocks value; portfolio needs non-Tech balance.
Triple-A balance sheet (one of only two, other being MSFT); best pipeline of potential blockbusters of any pharma; trades <20x earnings; quality healthcare at discount.
Well down from its highs with great balance sheet and diversified pharma/medical device portfolio; preferred healthcare play.
10 commercialized billion-dollar drugs, no major patent expirations, growing medtech via acquisitions (Shockwave, Abiomed). P/E 19x. Buy slowly; undervalued amid sector rotation.
Diversified pharma with triple-A balance sheet (better than US government) and robust new drug pipeline. Anchoring charitable trust through tech meltdown.
Shedding slow-growth divisions; focusing on high-growth pharmaceuticals with AAA balance sheet.
Talc litigation now manageable; pure pharma play with strong oncology franchise. July 15 earnings catalyst; rotation dislocation creates entry below 256.
Healthcare defensive rotation: stock jumped nearly $8 ahead of earnings as money fled hardware/components into full-immunity healthcare plays amid uncertainty.
